title: Local File or Web Request? Trace Two Different Journeys
summary: Compare a file URL with an HTTPS URL and trace exactly where storage, browser, network, server, request, and response responsibilities diverge.
objectives: Distinguish internet infrastructure, the web, browser behavior, and server behavior.
Diagnose a local success paired with a web response failure.

steps.title: Assign request and response · notice comparison
steps.instruction: In the offline-and-online notice comparison, which event belongs to the server side of the web exchange?
steps.why: Local-file access and web requests can display similar pages but fail at different boundaries. Working through assign request and response narrows the offline-and-online notice comparison while you defend the selected claim.

content.title: Assign request and response — offline-and-online notice comparison
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the offline-and-online notice comparison evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During offline-and-online notice comparison answer, identify which load journey boundary “Assign request and response” asks about.
Compare every offline-and-online notice comparison option with the named “Assign request and response” evidence during answer.
For offline-and-online notice comparison answer, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Assign request and response.”

options.text: Painting the received document into the learner’s browser window.
Returning an HTTP status, headers, and resource bytes to the browser.
Interpreting a pointer click on the learner’s input device.

steps.title: Rebuild project paths · notice comparison
steps.instruction: Within the offline-and-online notice comparison, reconstruct the project paths in causal order.
steps.why: Portable paths preserve the relationship between project resources when the containing folder moves. Ordering the offline-and-online notice comparison exposes its first unsupported causal handoff.

content.text: Do not order by vocabulary or visual position. Test whether each event creates the conditions required by the next event in the offline-and-online notice comparison.

hints: In the offline-and-online notice comparison sequence, locate the first event whose required input is not ready.
Separate offline-and-online notice comparison project paths events from later visible outcomes.
Rebuild the offline-and-online notice comparison sequence one causal handoff at a time.

options.text: Create one project root folder
Place the document and resource folders inside the root
Reference resources from the document with relative paths
Move the complete root and retest every resource

steps.title: Correct the mental model · notice comparison
steps.instruction: In the offline-and-online notice comparison, which statement about HTML is defensible?
steps.why: Separating system responsibilities makes a visible symptom traceable to the layer that can cause it. Working through correct the mental model narrows the offline-and-online notice comparison while you inspect the artifact.

content.title: Correct the mental model — offline-and-online notice comparison
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the offline-and-online notice comparison evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During offline-and-online notice comparison inspect, identify which system chain boundary “Correct the mental model” asks about.
Compare every offline-and-online notice comparison option with the named “Correct the mental model” evidence during inspect.
For offline-and-online notice comparison inspect, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Correct the mental model.”

options.text: HTML is executable machine code that runs without an application.
HTML is document data interpreted by a browser process running with operating-system and hardware support.
HTML is a physical display protocol sent directly to the monitor.

stimulus.title: System event trace · offline-and-online notice comparison
stimulus.caption: A simplified trace captured while a saved notice opens. Case: offline-and-online notice comparison; focus: correct the mental model.

steps.title: Distinguish internet and web · notice comparison
steps.instruction: In the offline-and-online notice comparison, which relationship is accurate?
steps.why: Local-file access and web requests can display similar pages but fail at different boundaries. Working through distinguish internet and web narrows the offline-and-online notice comparison before the result is shown.

content.title: Distinguish internet and web — offline-and-online notice comparison
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the offline-and-online notice comparison evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During offline-and-online notice comparison predict, identify which load journey boundary “Distinguish internet and web” asks about.
Compare every offline-and-online notice comparison option with the named “Distinguish internet and web” evidence during predict.
For offline-and-online notice comparison predict, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Distinguish internet and web.”

options.text: The web is the physical cables, while the internet is only the pages displayed in browsers.
The internet supplies network infrastructure; the web uses it to exchange linked resources through web protocols.
Internet and web are exact synonyms with no useful distinction.

steps.title: Read the real filename · notice comparison
steps.instruction: In the offline-and-online notice comparison, the file manager shows schedule.html, but its type is Plain Text and hidden extensions are enabled. What should be checked?
steps.why: Portable paths preserve the relationship between project resources when the containing folder moves. Working through read the real filename narrows the offline-and-online notice comparison as you connect the explanation to evidence.

content.text: A project is a directory tree. A filename includes its real extension, and a relative path describes one resource from the current document location. Keeping related resources inside the project and testing after moving the whole folder exposes machine-specific absolute paths and case mismatches. Apply this distinction during the offline-and-online notice comparison read task.

hints: During offline-and-online notice comparison read, identify which project paths boundary “Read the real filename” asks about.
Compare every offline-and-online notice comparison option with the named “Read the real filename” evidence during read.
For offline-and-online notice comparison read, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Read the real filename.”

options.text: Rename the containing folder to HTML so the operating system changes the file type.
Reveal full extensions because the file may actually be schedule.html.txt.
Assume the browser ignores extensions and opens every file as HTML.

steps.title: Locate a failure · notice comparison
steps.instruction: In the offline-and-online notice comparison, the file bytes are read, but no browser process starts. Which boundary should be investigated next?
steps.why: Separating system responsibilities makes a visible symptom traceable to the layer that can cause it. Working through locate a failure narrows the offline-and-online notice comparison while you inspect the artifact.

For offline-and-online notice comparison inspect, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Locate a failure.”

options.text: The CSS color selected by the page author.
The operating-system handoff that launches or addresses the browser application.
The monitor brightness setting, because no parsing event exists yet.

stimulus.title: System event trace · offline-and-online notice comparison
stimulus.caption: A simplified trace captured while a saved notice opens. Case: offline-and-online notice comparison; focus: locate a failure.

steps.title: Choose the next evidence · notice comparison
steps.instruction: In the offline-and-online notice comparison, the local file works, but the HTTPS URL returns 404. What should be inspected first?
steps.why: Local-file access and web requests can display similar pages but fail at different boundaries. Working through choose the next evidence narrows the offline-and-online notice comparison while you defend the selected claim.

content.title: Choose the next evidence — offline-and-online notice comparison
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the offline-and-online notice comparison evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

Compare every offline-and-online notice comparison option with the named “Choose the next evidence” evidence during answer.
For offline-and-online notice comparison answer, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Choose the next evidence.”

options.text: The local monitor cable, because the file rendered successfully.
The requested URL path and the server response evidence.
The HTML heading color, because status codes are produced by CSS.

steps.title: Write the evidence note · notice comparison
steps.instruction: Write a concise case note for the offline-and-online notice comparison. Explain the mechanism, cite observed evidence, and reject one plausible alternative.
steps.why: Explaining the offline-and-online notice comparison from memory exposes gaps that selected answers can hide. Cite the mechanism and its evidence.

content.title: Transfer brief
content.text: A teammate must be able to reproduce your conclusion without seeing the earlier answer choices. Name evidence and limits, not only the final verdict.

hints: Start the offline-and-online notice comparison case note with the earliest observed event.
Use these offline-and-online notice comparison technical anchors where they fit: URL, request, response, folder, relative path.
Close the offline-and-online notice comparison note by naming one alternative and the evidence that rules it out.
